summary: Epic Truckin' > The Other One jam, vivid He's Gone & Morning Dew
blurb: Second set builds from a mellow He’s Gone into a sprawling Truckin’ jam that evolves into The Other One, ending with an atmospheric Morning Dew. Jerry’s guitar weaves melodic leads while Phil supplies a clear bass tone and Keith adds subtle Rhodes texture. The soundboard capture is generally clear with good balance and definition.
review: June 29, 1973 at the Universal Amphitheatre captures one of the Dead’s most adventurous midsummer outings. Set 2 rolls out with a soulful “He’s Gone,” then rockets into a monster “Truckin’” that seamlessly transforms into an exploratory “The Other One.” The jam stretches, allowing Jerry to melt away the familiar Truckin theme with melodic leads and a wistful wah‑wah solo during “Playing In The Band,” while Phil’s Wes‑Montgomery‑style bass chords anchor the improvisation. Keith’s Fender Rhodes adds just enough color to keep the texture rich without overwhelming the mix. The night closes on an ethereal “Morning Dew,” leaving the audience with a lingering sense of wonder. The Cotsman soundboard source is generally clear and well‑balanced, offering solid bass (Bobby) and drum (Billy) definition despite minor muddiness in a few passages, making this recording the go‑to listen for anyone wanting to experience this fiery second‑set journey.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All language in the sole recording analysis is positive, describing an 'epic' Truckin’→The Other One jam, 'vivid' performances of He’s Gone and Morning Dew, and praising Jerry’s melodic leads and Phil’s clear bass tone. Consistent enthusiasm across multiple song mentions warrants a strong positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Generally clear soundboard with good balance, capturing the epic Truckin’→The Other One jam and detailed instrument definition.

key_highlights: Epic Truckin' → The Other One seamless segue and extended improvisation.
"He's Gone" opens second set with emotive phrasing.
"Morning Dew" provides a beautiful, atmospheric close to the night.
"Playing In The Band" features an introspective wah‑wah solo and subtle Rhodes texture.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar melts away the Truckin theme, delivering melodic leads in The Other One and an introspective wah‑wah solo on Playing In The Band.
band_performance.Phil: Phil (Bobby) receives praise for a clear bass tone and Wes‑Montgomery‑style chord work during The Other One.
band_performance.Brent: Keith’s Fender Rhodes adds a nice backing texture to the Playin' jam, enriching the overall sound without overpowering the mix.
band_performance.Billy: Billy’s drumming is described as nice and clear, contributing to the crispness of the recording.
